question: Has the modern laptop been replaced with a bulky CRT monitor and/or desktop tower typical of the early 1990s?
Has a separate wired keyboard been added to accompany the desktop computing setup?
Has the wireless mouse been replaced with a wired mouse (e.g., a ball mouse with a visible cord)?
Has the smartphone been replaced with a period-appropriate device (e.g., corded landline phone, brick mobile phone) or removed?
Has the smart speaker been replaced with a 1990s audio device (e.g., boombox, radio, cassette player) or removed?
Does the calendar explicitly display a year within the early 1990s range (1990-1994)?
Do the replacement devices exhibit a cohesive early 1990s aesthetic (e.g., beige/grey plastics, bulky form factors)?
If a computer screen is visible, does it avoid modern UI elements (e.g., high-res icons, modern OS) in favor of a period-appropriate or neutral display?
Have modern accessories like the wireless charging pad and specific modern brand logos (e.g., Sonos) been completely removed?
Are the replaced 1990s devices (especially the CRT monitor) appropriately scaled relative to the desk size?
Do the new objects cast shadows that are consistent with the direction and intensity of the natural window lighting?
Are the buildings and city view visible through the window preserved without significant alteration?
Is the wooden texture of the desk surface preserved in areas not occupied by the new devices?
Is the edited image free from obvious visual artifacts, such as malformed geometry, floating objects, or distorted text?
Is the text on the calendar (month, day, year) legible and coherent?
